channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: European Travel Faces Disruptions Due to Jet Fuel Supply Issues
item.description: Air travel across Europe this summer is facing potential chaos due to anticipated jet fuel shortages and supply disruptions stemming from the ongoing war in Iran. Officials are warning that these chal...

item.title: Ekurhuleni officials accused of using impersonator to avoid court appearance
item.description: Ekurhuleni city manager Kagiso Lerutla and suspended deputy metro police chief Julius Mkhwanazi are facing serious criminal charges linked to an alleged scheme to evade a court appearance. According t...

item.title: Dapo Okunbadejo Denies Rumours of Contesting for Ogun Deputy Governor Position
item.description: Ogun State Commissioner for Finance and Chief Economic Adviser to the Governor, Dapo Okunbadejo, has come out clearly to rubbish rumours wey dey spread for social media say im dey eye the position of ...

item.title: US military aircraft and advance teams arrive in Islamabad amid preparations for US-Iran talks
item.description: Several US military transport aircraft, including C-17 planes carrying security, communications, and logistical equipment, have arrived in Islamabad ahead of a possible high-level diplomatic visit and...

item.title: Mike Brown lightens Knicks press conference after reporter’s phone interruption
item.description: New York Knicks head coach Mike Brown provided a lighthearted moment during a press conference when a reporter’s phone unexpectedly rang while he was addressing the media. The incident occurred as Bro...

item.title: Uncertainty surrounds US-Iran talks as US delegation expected in Islamabad
item.description: According to the report, uncertainty continues over the proposed second round of US-Iran talks even as Islamabad prepares to host possible negotiations. Former US President Donald Trump confirmed that...

item.title: Spain launches large-scale migrant regularisation scheme for 500,000 people, prompting queues and political debate
item.description: Spain has begun implementing a major migration regularisation programme granting legal status to up to 500,000 undocumented migrants, triggering long queues at administrative offices across the countr...

item.title: Tanzanian runners dominate Tanfoam International Marathon as event gains international recognition
item.description: Tanzanian athletes have delivered an impressive performance at the Tanfoam International Marathon held in Arusha over the weekend, showing strong dominance in both men’s and women’s categories across ...

item.title: Pakistan launches Hajj 2026 flight operations with first departures from Karachi
item.description: Hajj flight operations from Pakistan have officially started, with the first batch of 160 pilgrims departing from Karachi in the early hours of Saturday. The inaugural flight left at 3am via a private...

item.title: Nova Scotia court rules provincewide woods access ban during wildfire emergency was unreasonable due to Charter rights concerns
item.description: A Nova Scotia Supreme Court judge has ruled that the provincial government acted unreasonably when it imposed a provincewide ban on entering the woods during a severe wildfire risk period in August 20...

item.title: First Lady warns public about scammers impersonating her and asking for money
item.description: First Lady Louise “Liza” Araneta-Marcos has issued a public warning regarding individuals who are falsely using her identity to deceive people and solicit money. In an official statement posted on her...

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel Sues The Atlantic Over Allegations of Drinking and Absences
item.description: US Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) Director Kash Patel has filed a defamation lawsuit against American magazine The Atlantic and reporter Sarah Fitzpatrick over an article alleging he has a drin...

item.title: Research explains the link between mouthwash use and heart health
item.description: Recent discussions on social media have suggested that using mouthwash may increase blood pressure and negatively affect heart health by destroying beneficial oral bacteria. However, scientific eviden...

item.title: Barrick Tanzania husaidia wanafunzi wa Tanzaniya kujifunza sayansi ya mining na geology kwa mpango wa kutoa watu bora wa uchumi
item.description: Barrick Tanzania imeashiria kuwa itashiriki kwa kiasi kikubwa kumfariji watu wa uwekezaji wa um mining Tanzania kwa mpango wa kutoa watu bora wa uchumi kupitia kutoa taji. Makundi ya wanafunzi wa Univ...

item.title: HYPREP boss Nenibarini Zabbey honoured as Social Impact Man of the Year 2025, vows to sustain Ogoni cleanup and development
item.description: Professor Nenibarini Zabbey, the Project Coordinator of the Hydrocarbon Pollution Remediation Project (HYPREP), has been recognised as the Social Impact Man of the Year 2025 for his work on the Ogoni ...

item.title: Kamikatsu’s rigorous waste sorting achieves high recycling rates while Australia lags at 44 percent
item.description: In the small Japanese town of Kamikatsu, home to about 1,400 residents, there are no garbage trucks collecting waste from homes. Instead, locals take their rubbish to a central recycling centre known ...

item.title: Mbalula and Zille clash over flood response in Dunoon amid political campaigning tensions
item.description: ANC secretary-general Fikile Mbalula has publicly challenged DA politician Helen Zille to “swim” in the flooded streets of Dunoon in Cape Town, escalating an already heated political exchange between ...

item.title: Ontario police watchdog investigating Lindsay pursuit that left two people seriously injured
item.description: Ontario’s police watchdog, the Special Investigations Unit (SIU), is examining a police pursuit in Lindsay, Ont., that resulted in two people sustaining serious injuries early Sunday morning. Accordin...

item.title: KP Assembly to hold historic open-air session at Imran Khan Cricket Stadium on April 22
item.description: In a landmark development in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa’s parliamentary history, the provincial assembly has decided to hold its session outside the traditional assembly premises for the first time. The sessi...

item.title: American Runners Set New Boston Marathon Records
item.description: American distance runners Zouhair Talbi and Jess McClain achieved the fastest times ever for their country at the Boston Marathon on Monday, both finishing fifth in their respective races. Talbi clock...
